prevalence.trend: stable; diagnosis increases with school age

burden.dalyPerCapita: low; high QOL and healthcare utilization impact

vulnerablePopulations: children with limited access to dietary counseling
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV functional constipation criteria; assess red flags for organic disease (failure to thrive, delayed meconium, neurologic deficits)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; symptom-driven diagnosis in APS
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: hundreds of thousands to millions of children
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with structured bowel regimen and family education

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in APS; referral for red flags or refractory constipation
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Education, toilet routine, fiber and fluids, osmotic laxatives (PEG/lactulose) and disimpaction regimens when needed
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to pediatric GI and inconsistent long-term follow-up and family adherence
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective low-cost management largely feasible in primary care
